subject: general-knowledge
description: Financial Bodies & International Organizations MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: What do the initials SEATO stand for?
mcqs.explanation: SEATO = South-East Asia Treaty Organization — founded by the Manila Pact 1955 to contain communism in SE Asia.

mcqs.q: SEATO was formally founded in:
mcqs.explanation: SEATO was formally founded in 1955 via the Manila Pact (signed 8 Sep 1954, in force 19 Feb 1955).

mcqs.q: SEATO was founded by which pact?
mcqs.explanation: The Manila Pact (signed in the Philippines on 8 September 1954) established SEATO.

mcqs.q: SEATO was founded by Australia, France, NZ, Pakistan, Philippines, Thailand, Britain and:
mcqs.explanation: The 8 founding SEATO members included the United States.

mcqs.q: SEATO's headquarters were in:
mcqs.explanation: SEATO was headquartered in Bangkok, Thailand.

mcqs.q: SEATO was dissolved in:
mcqs.explanation: SEATO was formally dissolved in 1977 after losing relevance post Vietnam War.

mcqs.q: What was the main purpose of SEATO?
options: Providing collective defence and enhancing economic co-operation in South-East Asia
Making SE Asia a free trade zone
Providing economic assistance to fight terrorism
mcqs.explanation: SEATO's primary purpose was collective defence (containment of communism) and enhanced economic cooperation in SE Asia.

mcqs.q: When did Pakistan withdraw from SEATO?
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan withdrew from SEATO in 1973 — following SEATO's failure to support Pakistan in the 1971 war with India.

mcqs.q: Where is the seat of the African Union?
mcqs.explanation: The African Union is headquartered in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia.

mcqs.q: When did the African Union come into existence?
mcqs.explanation: The African Union was launched on 26 May 2001, replacing the OAU.

mcqs.q: Which African organization was dissolved when the African Union was formed?
mcqs.explanation: The Organization of African Unity (OAU, 1963) was dissolved and replaced by the African Union in 2001-02.

mcqs.q: What are the aims of the African Union?
options: Unite the African member states politically, socially and economically
mcqs.explanation: The AU aims to unite African member states politically/socially/economically and is broadly modelled on the European Union.

mcqs.q: What is the official language of the African Union?
mcqs.explanation: The AU's working languages include Arabic, English, French, Portuguese, Spanish, Swahili and other African languages.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the African Union?
mcqs.explanation: The AU is headquartered in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia.

mcqs.q: What is the aim of the African Development Bank?
options: Promote economic and social development in the African region
Promote economic zone of African union
mcqs.explanation: The AfDB aims to promote economic and social development across the African region.

mcqs.q: How many regional members does the African Development Bank have?
mcqs.explanation: The AfDB has 53 regional African members.

mcqs.q: How many non-regional members does the African Development Bank have?
mcqs.explanation: The AfDB has 25 non-regional members (mainly OECD donors).

mcqs.q: What is the official language of the African Development Bank?
mcqs.explanation: The AfDB's two official working languages are English and French.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the African Development Bank?
mcqs.explanation: The AfDB is headquartered in Abidjan, Côte d'Ivoire.

mcqs.q: When was the Bank of Central African States formed?
mcqs.explanation: The Bank of Central African States (BEAC) was formed in 1973.

mcqs.q: Which is the official language of the Bank of Central African States?
mcqs.explanation: BEAC operates in French — the lingua franca of its francophone member states.

mcqs.q: What is the original number of member states in the Bank of Central African States?
mcqs.explanation: Five original members: Cameroon, CAR, Chad, Republic of Congo and Gabon.

mcqs.q: Who are the original members of the Bank of Central African States?
options: Chad and Republic of the Congo
mcqs.explanation: Original members: Cameroon, CAR, Chad, Republic of Congo and Gabon.

mcqs.q: Which European country is the guarantor of the Bank of Central African States?
mcqs.explanation: France guarantees the BEAC's CFA franc (Central African) currency.

mcqs.q: Which European country provides its currency framework for the Bank of Central African States?
mcqs.explanation: France backed the CFA franc, BEAC's regional currency — now pegged to the Euro.

mcqs.q: When did the East African Development Bank come into force?
mcqs.explanation: The East African Development Bank was established in 1967 under the Treaty for East African Cooperation.

mcqs.q: Through which treaty was the East African Development Bank originally established?
mcqs.explanation: Established under the Treaty for East African Cooperation, 1967.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the East African Development Bank?
mcqs.explanation: EADB is headquartered in Kampala, Uganda.

mcqs.q: Which is the official language of the East African Development Bank?
mcqs.explanation: EADB's official working language is English.

mcqs.q: When was the West African Development Bank formed?
mcqs.explanation: The West African Development Bank was formed in November 1973.

mcqs.q: Which is the official language of the West African Development Bank?
mcqs.explanation: The West African Dev Bank operates in French.

mcqs.q: When was the Central Bank of West African States (BCEAO) formed?
mcqs.explanation: BCEAO (Central Bank of West African States) was formed in 1962-1963 (commonly cited 1963).

mcqs.q: Which is the official language of the Central Bank of West African States?
mcqs.explanation: BCEAO operates in French — the official language of its 8 WAEMU members.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the Central Bank of West African States?
mcqs.explanation: BCEAO is headquartered at Avenue Abdoulaye Fadiga in Dakar, Senegal.

mcqs.q: In which year was the African Export and Import Bank (Afreximbank) established?
mcqs.explanation: Afreximbank was established in 1978 (commonly cited; established formally 1993 — using source's answer key).

mcqs.q: What is the official language of the African Export and Import Bank?
mcqs.explanation: Afreximbank operates in English, French, Arabic and Portuguese.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the African Export and Import Bank?
mcqs.explanation: Afreximbank is headquartered in Cairo, Egypt.

mcqs.q: When did the Economic Community of Central African States (ECCAS) come into existence?
mcqs.explanation: ECCAS was established in 1983 — covering 11 Central African states.

mcqs.q: In which year did the Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS) come into existence?
mcqs.explanation: ECOWAS was established by the Treaty of Lagos on 28 May 1975.

mcqs.q: Which is the official language of the Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS)?
mcqs.explanation: ECOWAS uses English, French and Portuguese as official languages.

mcqs.q: When was the West African Economic and Monetary Union (WAEMU/UEMOA) founded?
mcqs.explanation: WAEMU/UEMOA was founded in 1994.

mcqs.q: When was the Common Market for Eastern and Southern Africa (COMESA) formed?
mcqs.explanation: COMESA was formed in 1994 (often cited 1995) — replacing the PTA.

mcqs.q: How many member states are in the Common Market for Eastern and Southern Africa (COMESA)?
mcqs.explanation: COMESA has had varying membership — historically given as 28 in older sources (modern figure is 21).

mcqs.q: Which is the official language of the Common Market for Eastern and Southern Africa (COMESA)?
mcqs.explanation: Per the original answer key, COMESA's working language is given as French (though English is also widely used).

mcqs.q: How many member countries were founders of the Southern African Development Community (SADC)?
mcqs.explanation: SADC was founded in 1992 with 9 original member states.

mcqs.q: Which is the official language of the Southern African Development Community (SADC)?
mcqs.explanation: SADC uses English, French and Portuguese as official languages.

mcqs.q: When was the Lake Chad Basin Commission founded?
mcqs.explanation: The Lake Chad Basin Commission was founded on 22 May 1964.

mcqs.q: When was the Niger Basin Authority established?
mcqs.explanation: The Niger Basin Authority was established in 1980.

mcqs.q: When was the East African Community (EAC, revived) formed?
mcqs.explanation: The revived East African Community treaty was signed on 30 November 1999 (some sources cite 1998 for declaration).

mcqs.q: Who are the members of the East African Community?
mcqs.explanation: The 3 founding members of the revived EAC were Kenya, Uganda and Tanzania.

mcqs.q: When did the Inter-Governmental Authority on Development (IGAD) come into existence?
mcqs.explanation: IGAD was established on 21 March 1996.

mcqs.q: When was the International Organization for Migration (IOM) formed?
mcqs.explanation: IOM was founded as PICMME in Brussels in 1951.

mcqs.q: What is the official language of the International Organization for Migration (IOM)?
mcqs.explanation: IOM works in English, French and Spanish.

mcqs.q: In which city is the Red Cross based?
mcqs.explanation: The International Committee of the Red Cross is based in Geneva, Switzerland.

mcqs.q: When and where was the International Committee of the Red Cross (ICRC) established?
mcqs.explanation: The ICRC was established in Geneva in February 1863 (founded by Henri Dunant).

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the International Committee of the Red Cross?
mcqs.explanation: ICRC HQ is in Geneva, Switzerland.

mcqs.q: How many National Societies are there in the International Federation of Red Cross / Red Crescent?
mcqs.explanation: Per the older answer key the figure is 190 (currently 192).

mcqs.q: Why is World Red Cross and Red Crescent Day celebrated each year on 8 May?
options: On this day Red Cross was established
mcqs.explanation: 8 May was the birthday of Jean-Henri Dunant, founder of the Red Cross.

mcqs.q: When was the Red Cross established by Jean-Henri Dunant?
mcqs.explanation: The Red Cross movement was established in 1864 (the Geneva Convention; founding committee 1863).

mcqs.q: The Red Cross flag is the reverse of the flag of which country?
mcqs.explanation: The Red Cross emblem is a red cross on white — the reverse of the Swiss flag, honouring its Swiss founder.

mcqs.q: What is the motto of the Red Cross?
mcqs.explanation: The Red Cross motto is 'Service in War' (Inter arma caritas — 'Charity in war').

mcqs.q: When was Transparency International founded?
mcqs.explanation: Transparency International was founded in May 1993 by Peter Eigen.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of Transparency International?
mcqs.explanation: Transparency International is headquartered in Berlin, Germany.

mcqs.q: When was Amnesty International founded?
mcqs.explanation: Amnesty International was founded in July 1961 by British lawyer Peter Benenson.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of Amnesty International?
mcqs.explanation: Amnesty International is headquartered in London, UK.

mcqs.q: When did Amnesty International get the Nobel Peace Prize?
mcqs.explanation: Amnesty International received the Nobel Peace Prize in 1977.

mcqs.q: Who founded Amnesty International?
mcqs.explanation: Amnesty International was founded by British lawyer Peter Benenson in 1961.

mcqs.q: Amnesty International is associated with which field?
mcqs.explanation: Amnesty International campaigns globally for the protection of human rights.

mcqs.q: When was the Inter-Parliamentary Union (IPU) founded?
mcqs.explanation: The IPU was founded in 1889 — the world's oldest international political organization.

mcqs.q: Who founded the Inter-Parliamentary Union (IPU)?
mcqs.explanation: Per the source's answer key, William Randal Cremer founded the IPU (alongside Frédéric Passy).

mcqs.q: Which of the following pairs is NOT correct?
mcqs.explanation: SAARC's HQ is in Kathmandu, Nepal — NOT New Delhi. The other pairs are correct.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the Inter-Parliamentary Union (IPU)?
mcqs.explanation: The IPU is headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland.

mcqs.q: Which is the official language of the Islamic Development Bank?
mcqs.explanation: The Islamic Development Bank operates in Arabic, English and French.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the Islamic Development Bank?
mcqs.explanation: The Islamic Development Bank (IsDB) is headquartered in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia.

mcqs.q: When did the International Criminal Police Organization (INTERPOL) come into existence?
mcqs.explanation: INTERPOL was founded in 1923 in Vienna.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of INTERPOL?
mcqs.explanation: INTERPOL is headquartered in Lyon, France.

mcqs.q: What are the official languages of INTERPOL?
mcqs.explanation: INTERPOL uses 4 official languages: English, French, Spanish and Arabic.

mcqs.q: When did INTERPOL obtain observer status at the UN General Assembly?
mcqs.explanation: INTERPOL obtained observer status at the UN General Assembly on 15 October 1996.

mcqs.q: In which country is INTERPOL headquartered?
mcqs.explanation: INTERPOL is headquartered in France (Lyon).

mcqs.q: When was the International Mobile Satellite Organization (IMSO) set up?
mcqs.explanation: The International Mobile Satellite Organization (then INMARSAT) was set up in 1979.

mcqs.q: How many countries coordinate with INTERPOL (per source)?
mcqs.explanation: Per the original answer key, 153 countries (modern figure is ~196).

mcqs.q: When was the World Federation of Trade Unions (WFTU) founded?
mcqs.explanation: WFTU was founded in 1945, immediately after WWII.

mcqs.q: Where are the regional offices of the World Federation of Trade Unions?
mcqs.explanation: WFTU has regional offices in New Delhi, Havana, Dhaka, Damascus and Moscow.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the World Federation of Trade Unions?
mcqs.explanation: Per the original source, WFTU HQ was in Prague (Czech Republic). It later moved to Athens.

mcqs.q: When was the Inter-American Development Bank established?
mcqs.explanation: The IADB was established in 1959.

mcqs.q: The Inter-American Development Bank was formed to accelerate economic and social development of:
options: Both Latin America and the Caribbean
mcqs.explanation: The IADB serves both Latin America and the Caribbean.

mcqs.q: How many member countries are there in the Inter-American Development Bank?
mcqs.explanation: The IADB has 48 member countries.

mcqs.q: Which is the official language of the Inter-American Development Bank?
mcqs.explanation: IADB operates in English, French, Portuguese and Spanish.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the Inter-American Development Bank?
mcqs.explanation: IADB is headquartered in Washington D.C., USA.

mcqs.q: CARICOM is an organization related to Caribbean islands. What is its full form?
mcqs.explanation: CARICOM = Caribbean Community.

mcqs.q: CARICOM was established on 1 August 1973. Its headquarters are at:
mcqs.explanation: CARICOM is headquartered in Georgetown, Guyana.

mcqs.q: When was the Association of Caribbean States (ACS) formed?
mcqs.explanation: The Association of Caribbean States was formed on 24 July 1994.

mcqs.q: When did the Caribbean Community come into force?
mcqs.explanation: CARICOM came into force on 1 August 1973 (Treaty of Chaguaramas).

mcqs.q: Which is the official language of the Caribbean Community?
mcqs.explanation: CARICOM's working language is English.

mcqs.q: When was the Caribbean Development Bank established?
mcqs.explanation: The Caribbean Development Bank was established in 1969.

mcqs.q: How many regional members does the Caribbean Development Bank have?
mcqs.explanation: The CDB has 16 regional members.

mcqs.q: Which are the non-regional countries of the Caribbean Development Bank?
mcqs.explanation: The CDB's non-regional members include Italy, UK, Canada, China and Germany.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the Caribbean Development Bank?
mcqs.explanation: CDB is headquartered in Barbados.

mcqs.q: When was the Eastern Caribbean Central Bank established?
mcqs.explanation: The Eastern Caribbean Central Bank was established in 1983.

mcqs.q: Which is the official language of the Eastern Caribbean Central Bank?
mcqs.explanation: ECCB operates in English.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the Eastern Caribbean Central Bank?
mcqs.explanation: ECCB is headquartered in Basseterre, St. Kitts and Nevis.

mcqs.q: When was the Asian Development Bank (ADB) established?
mcqs.explanation: The ADB was established in 1966.

mcqs.q: What is the official language of the Asian Development Bank?
mcqs.explanation: ADB's working language is English.

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of the Asian Development Bank?
mcqs.explanation: ADB is headquartered in Manila, Philippines.

mcqs.q: Who is the current Director-General of the World Trade Organization (WTO)?
mcqs.explanation: Dr. Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala of Nigeria has been WTO Director-General since March 2021 — the first African and first woman.

mcqs.q: Who is the current Managing Director of the International Monetary Fund (IMF)?
mcqs.explanation: Kristalina Georgieva of Bulgaria has been IMF Managing Director since October 2019.

mcqs.q: Who is the current President of the World Bank?
mcqs.explanation: Ajay Banga (Indian-American) has been World Bank President since June 2023.

mcqs.q: Who is the current President of the BRICS New Development Bank (NDB)?
mcqs.explanation: Former Brazilian President Dilma Rousseff has been NDB President since March 2023.

mcqs.q: Who is the current President of the African Development Bank (AfDB)?
mcqs.explanation: Sidi Ould Tah (Mauritania) succeeded Akinwumi Adesina as AfDB President.

mcqs.q: Who is the current President of the Asian Development Bank (ADB)?
mcqs.explanation: Masato Kanda of Japan became ADB President in 2025.

mcqs.q: Who is the current President of the European Bank for Reconstruction and Development (EBRD)?
mcqs.explanation: Odile Renaud-Basso of France has been EBRD President since November 2020.

mcqs.q: Who is the current President of the Inter-American Development Bank (IADB)?
mcqs.explanation: Ilan Goldfajn of Brazil has been IADB President since December 2022.

mcqs.q: Who is the current President of the Islamic Development Bank (IsDB)?
mcqs.explanation: Dr. Muhammed Al-Jasser of Saudi Arabia is the current IsDB President.

mcqs.q: Which Nobel-laureate (1977) international organization for human rights was founded by Peter Benenson?
mcqs.explanation: Amnesty International, founded by Peter Benenson in 1961, won the Nobel Peace Prize in 1977.

mcqs.q: The Red Cross emblem (red cross on white) was inspired by which flag?
mcqs.explanation: The Red Cross emblem is the reverse of the Swiss national flag — honouring Swiss founder Henri Dunant.
